name: ML Pipeline
description: An end-to-end machine learning project covering data collection and preprocessing, model development, evaluation, and production deployment with monitoring. Use this template for predictive models, classifiers, recommendation systems, and NLP pipelines.

phases.name: Data Collection
phases.description: Identify, acquire, and document the raw data needed to train and evaluate the model. No training happens in this phase — only data sourcing and initial inspection.

epics.name: Data Source Identification
epics.description: Catalogue available data sources, assess their quality and licensing, and select the primary dataset.

stories.title: Document all candidate data sources and their access requirements
stories.description: Research available datasets (public benchmarks, internal databases, third-party APIs) and record their size, format, license, access method, and estimated data quality in a data-sources.md document.
completion_criteria: data-sources.md exists and contains at least 3 candidate sources with size, format, license, and access method documented for each
A recommendation section names the selected primary source with a written justification comparing it against the alternatives

stories.title: Obtain data access credentials and verify download
stories.description: Set up credentials, API keys, or database access for the selected data source. Verify a sample download works and document the access setup in the project README.
completion_criteria: A sample of at least 1,000 records is downloaded to data/raw/ and the file size matches the expected download size
The data access setup is documented in README.md with steps reproducible by a new team member

epics.name: Data Acquisition Pipeline
epics.description: Build a repeatable, automated pipeline that downloads and versions raw data.

stories.title: Write data download script with checksum verification
stories.description: Create scripts/download_data.py that downloads the full dataset, verifies its SHA-256 checksum against a stored expected value, and saves the raw files to data/raw/ with a timestamp suffix.
completion_criteria: Running `python scripts/download_data.py` downloads the dataset to data/raw/ and prints the file path and byte count
If the checksum does not match the expected value, the script exits with code 1 and deletes the corrupted file

stories.title: Generate initial Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA) notebook
stories.description: Create a Jupyter notebook at notebooks/01_eda.ipynb that loads the raw data, computes summary statistics (shape, dtypes, null counts, value distributions), and visualizes the target variable distribution.
completion_criteria: The notebook runs end-to-end without errors using `jupyter nbconvert --to notebook --execute`
The notebook includes: a markdown summary of dataset shape, a table of null counts per column, and a histogram of the target variable

epics.name: Data Quality Assessment
epics.description: Profile the dataset for quality issues that will affect model performance.

stories.title: Profile data quality and document findings
stories.description: Run a systematic data quality assessment covering: missing values, duplicates, outliers, class imbalance (for classification), and label quality. Document findings in data/quality-report.md.
completion_criteria: data/quality-report.md quantifies: the percentage of rows with at least one null, the exact duplicate row count, and the class distribution for the target variable
The report includes a recommended action for each identified quality issue (impute / drop / flag / resample)

phases.name: Preprocessing
phases.description: Transform raw data into model-ready feature matrices with a reproducible, testable pipeline.

epics.name: Feature Engineering
epics.description: Design and implement the feature extraction and transformation logic.

stories.title: Implement data cleaning pipeline with scikit-learn Pipeline
stories.description: Build a scikit-learn Pipeline that handles: missing value imputation (median for numeric, constant for categorical), outlier capping at the 1st and 99th percentile, and duplicate removal.
completion_criteria: Running the pipeline on the raw dataset produces a DataFrame with zero null values
The pipeline is wrapped in a Python class with `fit` and `transform` methods that are unit-tested in tests/test_preprocessing.py

stories.title: Engineer domain-specific features and encode categoricals
stories.description: Add domain-specific derived features, apply ordinal or one-hot encoding to categorical columns, and scale numeric features using StandardScaler or MinMaxScaler.
completion_criteria: The feature matrix produced by the full pipeline has no infinite or NaN values (verified by `np.isfinite(X).all()`)
The names and dtypes of all output features are documented in docs/features.md

epics.name: Train/Validation/Test Split
epics.description: Partition the dataset in a way that prevents data leakage and enables unbiased evaluation.

stories.title: Implement stratified train/validation/test split with fixed seed
stories.description: Create scripts/split_data.py that splits the preprocessed dataset 70/15/15 with stratification on the target variable and a fixed random seed. Save splits to data/splits/.
completion_criteria: The split script produces train.parquet, val.parquet, and test.parquet with row counts matching 70/15/15% of the total (within 1 row rounding)
The target class distribution in each split matches the full dataset distribution within 2 percentage points

phases.name: Model Development
phases.description: Train, compare, and tune candidate models. Select the best performer for productionization.

epics.name: Baseline Model
epics.description: Establish a simple baseline to measure all subsequent models against.

stories.title: Train and evaluate a majority-class or linear baseline
stories.description: Train a simple baseline model (majority class classifier or logistic regression) and log its performance metrics to MLflow. This sets the floor that every subsequent model must beat.
completion_criteria: The baseline model's metrics (accuracy, F1, AUC-ROC where applicable) are logged to an MLflow run named 'baseline'
A baseline-results.md file records the metrics and the rationale for choosing this baseline type

epics.name: Candidate Model Training
epics.description: Train and compare multiple model families to identify the best-performing architecture.

stories.title: Train gradient boosting model with hyperparameter search
stories.description: Train an XGBoost or LightGBM model using Optuna for hyperparameter optimization over 50 trials. Log all trials to MLflow.
completion_criteria: The best trial's validation metric exceeds the baseline's validation metric by at least 2 percentage points
All 50 Optuna trials are logged as child runs under an MLflow experiment named 'gradient-boosting'

stories.title: Train neural network model and compare to gradient boosting
stories.description: Train a PyTorch or scikit-learn MLP, evaluate on the validation set, and produce a comparison table of all candidate models in models/comparison.md.
completion_criteria: models/comparison.md contains a table comparing at least 3 model variants on validation accuracy, F1, training time, and inference latency
The selected model is named and justified based on the comparison criteria defined in the project goal

phases.name: Evaluation
phases.description: Rigorously evaluate the selected model on held-out test data, analyze failure modes, and document limitations.

epics.name: Test Set Evaluation
epics.description: Run the final model on the held-out test set and produce the official evaluation report.

stories.title: Evaluate selected model on test set and generate evaluation report
stories.description: Run inference on the held-out test set, compute all relevant metrics, generate confusion matrix and ROC curve visualizations, and write the evaluation report to docs/evaluation-report.md.
completion_criteria: docs/evaluation-report.md includes: test set accuracy, precision, recall, F1, and AUC-ROC (for classifiers) or RMSE and MAE (for regressors)
The report includes a confusion matrix heatmap saved to docs/figures/confusion-matrix.png

stories.title: Perform error analysis on misclassified samples
stories.description: Identify the top-50 highest-confidence errors, examine them for patterns (e.g., specific feature ranges, data quality issues, edge cases), and document findings.
completion_criteria: docs/error-analysis.md describes at least 3 distinct error patterns identified in the misclassified samples
Each pattern includes a count of affected samples and a proposed mitigation (data collection, feature engineering, or model adjustment)

epics.name: Bias and Fairness Audit
epics.description: Check model performance across demographic subgroups to identify disparate impact.

stories.title: Compute performance metrics disaggregated by protected attributes
stories.description: Identify relevant protected attributes in the dataset, compute the primary metric (accuracy or F1) separately for each subgroup, and flag any subgroup where performance falls more than 5 percentage points below the overall metric.
completion_criteria: docs/fairness-audit.md reports the primary metric for each subgroup of each identified protected attribute
Any subgroup with performance more than 5pp below average is flagged with a REVIEW REQUIRED label

phases.name: Deployment
phases.description: Package the trained model as a serving API, deploy to the target environment, and set up monitoring.

epics.name: Model Serving API
epics.description: Wrap the trained model in a REST API for real-time inference.

stories.title: Implement FastAPI inference endpoint
stories.description: Create a FastAPI application with a POST /predict endpoint that accepts feature input as JSON, runs the preprocessing pipeline and model inference, and returns a prediction with a confidence score.
completion_criteria: POST /predict with valid input returns HTTP 200 with `{"prediction": <value>, "confidence": <float 0-1>}` in under 200ms
POST /predict with missing required feature fields returns HTTP 422 with a field-level validation error

stories.title: Containerize the model server and push to registry
stories.description: Write a Dockerfile for the FastAPI inference server, build the image, run the test suite against the containerized version, and push to the container registry.
completion_criteria: The Docker image builds in under 5 minutes and the container starts and passes the health check within 30 seconds
The image is pushed to the configured registry and the image digest is recorded in deployment/image-digests.md

epics.name: Monitoring and Drift Detection
epics.description: Set up production monitoring to detect data drift, prediction drift, and performance degradation.

stories.title: Implement prediction logging and statistical drift alerts
stories.description: Log every prediction request (features + prediction, no PII) to a structured store. Set up a daily job that computes the Population Stability Index (PSI) for each input feature and alerts when PSI > 0.2.
completion_criteria: Every prediction request writes a log entry containing the feature values (excluding PII fields), the prediction, confidence, and timestamp
The daily drift check script exits with code 1 and prints the PSI value and feature name when PSI exceeds 0.2 for any feature
